rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/24/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No proof of service of notice of the "Errata" filed on 4/21/22 has been filed. [The legislature has determined that petitions may be SUPPLEMENTED to provide additional information or AMENDED to correct or modify information; an "errata" is not recognized by the legislature. [CRC 7.3.]] The "Errata" was not signed and verified by the executor of the will. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] A courtesy copy of the executor's verification of the "Errata", a courtesy copy of the proof of service of notice of the "Errata", and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/15/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SARA R. JULIAN 2018 DATED 1/16/19 SPECIAL NEEDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. No courtesy copy of the proof of service. No original bank statements. The amount of bond is incorrect; it appears that bond should be INCREASED, based on the FMV of assets on hand as of 2/28/22. The market value of assets on 2/29/20 differs from the value shown at the end of the first amended account. Please explain the securities received from the trust on 3/2/20 and 4/7/20. They were not reflected in the third amended accounting filed in that trust case on 5/21/21 or in the receipt filed on 10/13/21. Who is/are the original payee(s) of the personal expenses shown on page 22 of the accounting? Schedule F does not show the number of shares of each security. Fees and costs will be reviewed together with the related irrevocable trust. (The orders in the related cases were dated 11/15/21, not 11/16/21 or 11/18/21.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/22/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Recovery Of Trust Property Held By Former Trustee; For Double Damages; For Attorney'S Fees And Costs

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RUTHIE OLAFSEN OIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To Execute And Fund Trust And Execute Pour Over Will By Substituted Judgment, Peition To Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; And Petition To Set Trustee'S Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/27/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The judicial officer issued a note saying Exhibit A must identify all assets of the trust, particularly in light of the confusing nature of the several I&As on file. The court will not grant language that all accounts held at an institution and must state the specific account numbers for each institution. It appears #9 and #10 should be removed. Unclear why conservatorship assets listed on the 04/26/2022 I&A Book entry shares at AST and EQ Shareholder Services Account are not listed on Exhibit A. 2) Judicial officer will determine at hearing whether to approve the new language in V(I) that states period payments of compensation on account, subject to the requirements of Probate Code section 2643 and rule 7.755 are allowed. 3) Judicial officer will determine at hearing whether to approve the new language in VI(F) that states the Trustee may pay himself periodic payments of compensation on account, subject to the requirements of Probate Code section 2643 and California Rule of Court 7.755. 4) The judicial officer issued a note regarding section VI(E) contemplates that the trust may continue well past the settlor's death. Consider revising this section to provide a course of action in the event of a minor beneficiary that does not depend on the continued existence of this trust. It appears the section was changed with intention to create a UTMA account with a suitable adult as custodian. Judicial officer will determine whether to approve this language as the court is not inclined to grant this sizeable distribution to a custodian account and generally requires an appointed guardian.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Deteriming Invalidity Of Purported Trust And Imposing Constructive Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/24/22 to be heard by Judge Quinn. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) that must be addressed before the hearing: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Who are the parties listed in paragraph 87 of the petition to whom notice was given in care of another? Are they minors? If so, how old are the minors? The person to whom notice was mailed is presumably the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides, but the petition does not disclose this. [CRC 7.51(d) and Probate Code Sect. 1460.1.] The proof of service of the amendment shows that notice was mailed on 3/2/22, but the amendment was not filed until 5/11/22. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/15/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
